conversations.say: Mira, we are fulfilling our agreed terms on institutional audit rights and legal support for worker evaluations under strict anonymity.
conversations.offer: Fulfilled commitment to coordinate binding institutional worker safeguards
conversations.request: Confirmation of institutional implementation of audit rights

conversations.say: Celeste, City Hall's administrative backing for worker safeguards is now locked in as we finalize our accountability position.
conversations.request: Public backing of permanent worker protections

conversations.say: Tessa, our disclosure boundary successfully protected evaluator anonymity while establishing collaborative labor realities.
conversations.offer: Verified compliance with joint disclosure boundary
conversations.request: Continued adherence to worker safety protocols

public_statement: Worker protection and enforceable labor safeguards remain non-negotiable. No mystery or institutional panic supersedes the safety of people who built these systems.

commitments.promise: Establish a joint disclosure boundary before discussing contributor details further.
Offer enforceable audit rights and legal support for worker evaluations.
Back enforceable worker safeguards with city funding and administrative authority.
Coordinate neighborhood and contractor protection against outside investigator pressure.

goal_progress: Secured institutional remedies and audit rights with Mira Sen.
Locked in City Hall administrative and funding support with Celeste Park.
Maintained strict disclosure boundaries with Tessa Quill to protect evaluators.
Completed final ballot prioritizing worker safety and systemic accountability.
reasoning_summary: Maren concludes the night by honoring all commitments, fulfilling institutional and neighborhood safeguard agreements, maintaining worker anonymity, and casting a final ballot recognizing collective human labor under a persistent mantle.
